overview: This deck leverages a high-volume token generation engine centered around instant and sorcery spellcasting. By repeatedly triggering abilities from Yorion, Sky Nomad and Sea Gate Restoration, you flood the battlefield with flying Bird tokens. Quintorius Kand acts as both a secondary token source and a massive threat that scales with your spellcasting density.
win_conditions: Overwhelm the opponent with a massive army of flying Bird tokens generated by instant and sorcery casting
Grow Quintorius Kand into an unblockable stat stick while generating additional tokens
Use spell-triggered token generation to bypass board wipes and recover quickly

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more cheap instants or sorceries to trigger token generation
Hands containing Quintorius Kand or Yorion, Sky Nomad with early triggers
Hands with Sea Gate Restoration and multiple spells to cast
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including duals/fixing), Quintorius, and at least one cantrip or removal spell.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking instant or sorcery spells to fuel the engine
Hands with only high-curve creatures and no triggers
Hands missing lands or spell interaction
Mulligan to hands with 3+ lands and no spells, or hands with multiple copies of the same low-impact card.

early_game: Prioritize casting cheap instants and sorceries to trigger Sea Gate Restoration and early Yorion abilities
Play lands efficiently to ensure you can cast Quintorius Kand by turn four
Avoid trading tokens into opponent removal unless necessary
Turn 1: Play a land, ideally a dual or shock land if possible, or a basic that fixes into your next turn.
Turn 2: Cast a cantrip or removal to stabilize, or play Quintorius if you have mana for it.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your second spell on turn 2 to trigger Yorion if possible, or set up Quintorius for turn 3.
mid_game: Cast Quintorius Kand and Yorion, Sky Nomad to accelerate token production
Use removal and counterspells to protect your engine while generating more Birds
Begin attacking with flying tokens to apply pressure while building board presence
Use Quintorius to cycle through your deck, but avoid discarding key combo pieces or removal.
Prioritize casting instants/sorceries to trigger Yorion, but balance with board presence.
Manage graveyard carefully if facing graveyard strategies.
Commit threats when you have interaction or a board wipe ready.
Hold back if the opponent has unanswered removal or counterspells.
Use Yorion tokens to trade efficiently and apply pressure.
late_game: Flood the board with tokens to overwhelm opponent board wipes and removal
Pump Quintorius Kand with every noncreature spell to create a massive threat
Close out the game with a massive flying token army that cannot be blocked efficiently
Sea Gate Restoration (creates two flying Spirits), Yorion (generates tokens from spell casting), and Quintorius (digging for the final piece).
Win by overwhelming the board with flying Spirits or out-resourcing the opponent.
Go all-in when you have multiple cantrips/removal in hand, a clear path to cast two spells, and the opponent has no sweepers.
Commit when you can trigger Yorion reliably and have enough interaction to protect your board.

key_cards.name: Quintorius Kand
key_cards.role: Primary threat and secondary token engine
key_cards.notes: Cast on curve and trigger with every noncreature spell to grow stats and generate Birds. Protect it from removal as it becomes a massive blocker and attacker.

key_cards.name: Yorion, Sky Nomad
key_cards.notes: Use the land search ability to fix mana or accelerate. Trigger the instant and sorcery ability repeatedly to flood the board with flying tokens.

key_cards.name: Sea Gate Restoration
key_cards.notes: Play early to trigger on every instant and sorcery. Synergizes heavily with Yorion and cheap spells to establish board presence quickly.

sequencing_tips: Cast Sea Gate Restoration before your first instant or sorcery to maximize early triggers
Hold removal in hand to trigger Quintorius Kand while protecting your threats
Prioritize casting instants and sorceries before committing creatures to the battlefield to maximize token generation
Use countermagic to deny opponent interaction while still triggering your own token abilities
Quintorius's ability is a draw/discard/play loop, so manage your graveyard carefully.
Yorion requires casting two spells per turn, so sequence your turns to maximize token generation.
Sea Gate Restoration is a powerful finisher but vulnerable to removal
protect it or cast it when safe.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without interaction.
Watch for opponent's sideboard cards that target your strategy.

matchups.advice: Focus on early token generation with Sea Gate Restoration and cheap spells to stabilize. Use flying Birds to bypass early threats and protect Quintorius Kand with counterspells.
Push damage with flying tokens early before they stabilize. Use multiple cheap spells to trigger abilities and overwhelm their hand size. Protect Quintorius Kand with counterspells.
Race them by generating tokens on curve. Use removal efficiently to clear their threats while maintaining your token engine. Avoid trading your engine pieces for their creatures.
Use cantrips to dig past counters, cast spells in response to counterspells, or use cheap spells to trigger Yorion. Consider sideboarding in spell Pierce or similar if available.
Apply early pressure with cantrips and removal. Use sweepers to clear their large threats. Out-value them with card advantage from Quintorius.
Win the resource war. Prioritize card advantage, use Quintorius to dig deeper, and time your Yorion triggers to out-value their token generation. Sideboard in additional removal and counters.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, counterspells, and targeted removal.
Note when opponents save removal for your key spells.
Track graveyard activity if facing Niv to Light or Greasefang.
